message:
fix(stdlib_abi): use mutable static storage for basename/dirname "." fallback

The previous implementation returned `b".\0".as_ptr() as *mut c_char`
which points to read-only .rodata memory. If a POSIX caller modified
the returned pointer (which is legal per spec), this would cause UB
or SIGSEGV.

Fix: Use separate static Mutex<[u8; 2]> buffers (BASENAME_DOT, DIRNAME_DOT)
for the "." fallback, ensuring the returned pointer is always to mutable
memory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
